title: Best Machine Washable Dog Beds (2026) – Easy-Clean Picks Tested
seoTitle: Best Machine Washable Dog Beds (2026) – Easy Clean
seoDescription: Machine washable dog beds tested for wash durability, shrink resistance & drying time. Beds that stay comfortable after 50+ washes. Updated 2026.
h1Override: Best Machine Washable Dog Beds – Easy-Clean Picks That Last
excerpt: Machine washable dog beds tested for durability after repeated washing. Best picks that don't shrink, pill, or lose shape after 50+ wash cycles.
quickAnswer: The best machine washable dog bed has a removable zippered cover made from pre-shrunk polyester or canvas fabric, reinforced stitching that withstands 50+ wash cycles, and a waterproof inner liner protecting the foam core from moisture damage during accidents between washes.
featuredSnippet: The best machine washable dog beds use removable zippered covers made from pre-shrunk polyester or canvas. Look for reinforced double-stitching, heavy-duty zippers, and a waterproof inner liner. Wash covers weekly on gentle cycle with pet-safe detergent. Avoid fabric softener — it reduces absorbency and can irritate dog skin.

sections.heading: Why Washability Is the Most Underrated Dog Bed Feature
sections.content:
Dog beds accumulate dirt, dander, hair, bacteria, and odor faster than most owners realize. Within 2 weeks of regular use, an unwashed dog bed harbors more bacteria per square inch than a kitchen floor. For dogs with allergies, skin conditions, or compromised immune systems, a dirty bed actively worsens their health.

Yet washability is the feature most dog owners overlook when buying a bed. They focus on foam thickness, design, and price — then discover their $150 bed has a non-removable cover that can't be machine washed.

The best dog beds make cleaning effortless: a zippered removable cover, pre-shrunk fabric, and a waterproof inner liner that protects the foam from accidents between washes. For a complete comparison of all dog bed types by feature, see our [best dog beds guide](/guides/best-dog-bed-2026).

sections.heading: Best Washable Dog Beds by Type
sections.content:
### Removable Cover Beds (Best Overall)

Beds with a zippered cover that lifts off for machine washing while the foam insert stays dry. This is the gold standard. Look for:
- Heavy-duty YKK or equivalent zippers
- Pre-shrunk polyester or canvas fabric
- Double-stitched seams that hold up to agitation cycles

### Fully Washable Beds (Best for Puppies)

Some smaller beds are designed to go entirely into the washing machine — cover, fill, and all. These work well for small dogs and puppies but aren't practical for large orthopedic foam beds. Memory foam should never go in a washing machine.

### Waterproof Beds (Best for Seniors)

Beds with waterproof-coated outer fabric that can be wiped clean between washes. Ideal for senior dogs prone to incontinence. The downside: waterproof fabrics can feel plasticky and some dogs avoid them. Pair with a soft blanket on top.

For dogs needing both washability and orthopedic support, see our [orthopedic dog bed guide](/guides/best-orthopedic-dog-bed) — all top picks feature removable washable covers.

sections.heading: How to Wash a Dog Bed Properly
sections.content:
**Weekly cover wash routine:**
1. Remove the zippered cover
2. Pre-treat stains with pet-safe enzyme cleaner
3. Wash on gentle cycle, cold or warm water (never hot — causes shrinking)
4. Use pet-safe detergent — no fabric softener (reduces absorbency, irritates skin)
5. Tumble dry low or air dry

**Monthly deep clean:**
1. Vacuum the foam insert to remove embedded hair and dander
2. Spot-clean foam stains with diluted enzyme cleaner
3. Air out the foam insert in indirect sunlight for 2–4 hours
4. Check the waterproof liner for cracks or wear

**What NOT to do:**
- Never machine wash memory foam — it absorbs water and takes days to dry, growing mold
- Never use bleach — it degrades foam and irritates dog skin
- Never use high heat drying — it shrinks covers and damages waterproof coatings

Active dogs who get dirty from [outdoor enrichment games](/guides/outdoor-dog-games-2026) need more frequent washing. Consider having two covers and rotating weekly.

faq.question: Can you put a dog bed in the washing machine?
faq.answer: You can machine wash removable dog bed covers on a gentle cycle with pet-safe detergent. Never machine wash memory foam inserts — they absorb water and develop mold. Small polyester-fill beds can go in the machine entirely if they fit.

faq.question: How often should you wash a dog bed?
faq.answer: Wash the removable cover weekly. Spot-clean stains immediately. Vacuum the foam insert monthly. Deep-clean the entire bed quarterly. Dogs with allergies, skin conditions, or that play outdoors daily may need more frequent washing.

faq.question: What detergent is safe for dog beds?
faq.answer: Use fragrance-free, dye-free pet-safe laundry detergent. Avoid fabric softener — it reduces absorbency and leaves residue that can irritate dog skin. Enzyme-based cleaners work best for pre-treating urine and drool stains.

faq.question: Do washable dog beds shrink?
faq.answer: Cheap covers shrink significantly after 3–5 washes. Quality pre-shrunk polyester and canvas covers maintain their dimensions. Always wash on cold or warm — never hot — and tumble dry on low heat. Air drying eliminates shrinkage risk entirely.

faq.question: How do you get dog smell out of a bed?
faq.answer: Enzyme-based pet odor eliminators break down the organic compounds causing the smell. Sprinkle baking soda on the foam insert, let sit 30 minutes, then vacuum. If odor persists after treatment, the foam is saturated — this is why waterproof liners are essential.
